LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER BAKER'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEPOSITIONS OF DEFENDANTS AND FOR MONETARY AND OTHER SANCTIONS - MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. THE DEFENDANTS' OPPOSITION DOES NOT EFFECTIVELY DISPUTE THE NUMEROUS INSTANCES IN WHICH PLAINTIFF ATTEMPTED TO SET A DEPOSITION DATE OVER MANY MONTHS, WHICH CULMINATED IN THIS MOTION. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summ Adj; Evidence
Off Calendar Sep-10-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALAN COHEN APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KEVIN D. REESE & ATTY. TIMOTHY L. REED OF OGLETREE DEAKINS, TEL. NO. 536-3407 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DIGNITY HEALTH, ADELWISA MAXIE, GLORIA LAU and SAINT FRANCIS MEMORIAL HOSPITAL's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Summary Adjudication - Motion for summary judgment or adjudication is denied. As to the defamation and false light causes of action, triable issues of material fact exist. First, as to the email sent by Defendant Adelwisa Maxie to four union members, triable issues of material fact as to Maxie's motivation for sending the email. Based on the plain language of the email, a reasonable jury could find that Maxie sent the email to vent about her feeling and experience to her colleagues, and would therefore not be entitled to the common interest privilege. (UMF 144-145, 147, 166.) Additionally, triable issues of material fact remain as to whether Defendants' failure to conduct an investigation as to who left the voicemail amounts to recklessness. (UMF 124, 126, 128-136.) "The mere profession of a defendant that he believed in good faith that his statements were true does not automatically entitle him to a verdict in his favor'.[r]eckless is, after all, only negligence raised to a higher power'[f]urther actual malice may be inferred when the investigation was grossly inadequate under the circumstances." (Fisher v. Larsen (1982) 138 Cal.App.3d 627, 640.) As to the punitive damages claim, triable issues of material fact exist. Determining whether an individual is a "managing agent" under Cal. Civ. Code sec. 3294(b) is "a question of fact for decision on a case-by-case basis." (White v. Ultramar, Inc. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 563, 566-67.) The evidence establishes that a reasonable jury could find that Defendant Gloria Lau exercised "a substantial independent authority and judgment in [her] corporate decisionmaking so that [her] decisions ultimately determine corporate policy," given that the evidence establishes that Lau had the authority to email all nursing staff about the voicemail, as well as have Plaintiff's picture posted throughout the hospital. (UMF 111, 172.) Further, there is a triable issue of material fact as to whether the Vice President of Human Resource's ratified Lau's conduct, given that the Vice President knew that Lau sent the email, but did not send, or instruct Lau to send, a follow up email stating that Defendants were still investigating who left the voicemail. (UMF 178-179. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Plaintiff Christopher Baker'S Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants And For Monetary And Other Sanctions (Continued From Aug-11-14)
Off Calendar Aug-29-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALAN F. COHEN, TEL. NO. 984-1943 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. KEVIN D. REESE & ATTY. TIMOTHY L. REED OF OGLETREE/DEAKINS, TEL. NO. 536-3407 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
ALICE CHANG OF JAN BROWN ASSOCIATES, TEL. NO. 981-3498, COURT REPORTER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Plaintiff Christopher Baker's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants And For Monetary And Other Sanctions - Motion to compel is granted and Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Sanctions in the amount of $5,000.00 are awarded against Defendants Saint Francis Memorial Hospital and Dignity Health. The Defendants' opposition does not effectively dispute the numerous instances in which Plaintiff attempted to set a deposition date over many months, which culminated in this motion. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: John Stewart; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Alice Chang, CSR # 13654 =302/JS
